Triggering the system-level rural small workshop deconstruction and the '2-personnel village/town scrap wire stripping/drawing workshop and scrap plastic recycling point' validation probe. The highly incongruous Apple consumer electronics OEM template in the original data has been physically completely written off. This enterprise, named 'Badatong Cable,' was mechanically categorized into 'Electrical Machinery and Equipment Manufacturing' by the official system. Its business license claims 'wire and cable, aluminum and copper wire drawing, high and low voltage distribution cabinet manufacturing.' However, the forensic logic engine directly penetrated this heavy industry disguise, capturing an extremely poignant physical baseline: first, its paid-in capital is a pitiful 100,000 RMB! The insured headcount is only 2 people! The most fatal judgment comes from its highly concrete spatial coordinates: 'Xiaoqian Village, Heling Community, Tangshan Subdistrict, Jiangning District.' The logical deduction instantly turned into an industrial sketch of China's rural bottom tier: in a village (Xiaoqian Village) in the far suburbs of Nanjing, a boss with his wife or a relative (2 people), holding 100,000 RMB, absolutely cannot build a modern factory producing high-voltage distribution cabinets and formal cables. Combined with the 'aluminum and copper wire drawing, plastic particle manufacturing' in its license, the truth is starkly naked and full of the pungent smell of plastic: this is a 100% pure 2-Employee Rural Wire Recycling & Plastic Pelletizing Workshop (a village-level family workshop). They highly likely bought a second-hand wire stripping machine or a small wire drawing machine in a village courtyard or an illegal shed, specializing in recycling scrap cables, pulling out the copper and aluminum wires inside (wire drawing) to sell as scrap metal; they melt and shred the stripped wire insulation and other scrap plastics using crude machines to make low-end 'plastic particles' to sell to downstream small factories. The official 'Electrical Manufacturing Entity' tag is the most outrageous elevation of this village-level rough scrap processing workshop operating on the fringes of environmental protection.
